title: The Desert March Continues
description: On pages 5–7, this part focuses on the desert march continues, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Desert March Continues. The Straw Hats struggle across Alabasta’s harsh desert as Vivi’s urgency and the scale of the rebellion become clearer. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Hey, it's not our fault you lost at rock-paper- scissors and have to carry all our stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Make sure not to drop our bags!

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: Finally, a shade! Awww... it feels great!

dialogue.speaker: Toto
dialogue.text: Huh? Why's he running back here?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: There're some injured birds over here!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Aghh! Our stuff is all gone!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: And all the birds that were here just a moment ago are gone too!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Those were swindle-herons. They trick passing travellers to steal their luggage for food and water.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's my fault for not warning you earlier...

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: Herons pretending to be hurt...? What a flock of swindlers!

summary: This part develops the desert march continues and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
